plan: In this tree, SQLCompiler.get_group_by (compiler.py ~line 128) extends GROUP BY with expr.get_group_by_cols() for each non-ref ORDER BY item; OrderBy.get_group_by_cols recurses into its sources, and Random (django/db/models/functions/math.py, ~line 144) inherits BaseExpression.get_group_by_cols, which returns [self], injecting RANDOM() into GROUP BY. Fix where the state is produced, not in the compiler consumer: override Random.get_group_by_cols(alias=None) to return [], since a per-row random value can never legitimately partition groups. This keeps the fix single-file and backend-agnostic (Random already vendors its as_mysql/as_oracle/as_sqlite branching), rather than special-casing isinstance(exp, Random) inside get_group_by as the reporter's compiler patch does.
